knowledgeScope.topic: Inspire or improve economic development, innovation and entrepreneurship policies and/or national strategies

programImpact.label: Influence or strengthen economic development, innovation and entrepreneurship strategies, initiatives, and projects

Expand your regional and international network of high-level decision-makers to advance your projects, enhance your events and or better position your company or institution in the Americas and beyond
Enhance your skills in economic development, innovation and entrepreneurship to further support the national or subnational economic development agenda
Learn about economic development best practices that can be replicated in your institution, your country and/or region
Better position your company or institution in the Americas and beyond
Learn about business soft-landing opportunities in Michigan, U.S, and/or in other countries

qualitativeQuotes.name: Erick Marín Muller
qualitativeQuotes.text: Made valuable relationships with participants with whom we will try to establish working relations in the near future.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Carlos Fernando Villacreses Sánchez
qualitativeQuotes.text: Networking, nuevas experiencias para implementar programas en la universidad y fortalecer el ecosistema de innovación y emprendimiento

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alejandro Echeverri
qualitativeQuotes.text: Assist ACE fellows startups to understand and validate business opportunities in the Colombian ecosystem Assist the government of Cordoba (Argentina) to validate the opportunity to open a government representation in US, utilizing ProColombia’s experience Validate investment and business opportunities with the many companies we visited during the agenda this week.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Narine Haroyan
qualitativeQuotes.text: Collaborations between universities, private and public sectors

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alfredo E. Lagleyze
qualitativeQuotes.text: Networking and connectiosn through the program were great.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Erick Marín Muller
qualitativeQuotes.text: Yes, the knowledge content of the program was very enriching!

qualitativeQuotes.name: Carlos Fernando Villacreses Sánchez
qualitativeQuotes.text: Sin duda alguna, el compartir conocimientos ha generado varias ideas que “tropicalizadas” pueden implementarse de inmediato y empezar a generar resultado en el mediano plazo

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alejandro Echeverri
qualitativeQuotes.text: Definitely, the program helped me to better understand best practices for a government entity such as ProColombia to work and collaborate with the private sector and the academia

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alfredo E. Lagleyze
qualitativeQuotes.text: Yes, in many different ways and on a diverse subjects. With contact with entrepreneurial projects and startups, non profit organizations and traditional plus advanced companies visits and presentations.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Ronaldo Alexandre Barquette
qualitativeQuotes.text: Yes. I could see what American companies are innovating and creating value in their sectors.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Carlos Fernando Villacreses Sánchez
qualitativeQuotes.text: Si, convenios de transferencia de metodologías y organización de misiones para intercambios cortos de alumnos /docentes

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alejandro Echeverri
qualitativeQuotes.text: With government of Cordoba (explained above) a collaboration to assist them to understand how to establish and open a government entity in US. Assist Jared and Issac with their endeavors to validate the Colombian market

qualitativeQuotes.name: Narine Haroyan
qualitativeQuotes.text: We hope to bring some participants to ACE Armenia and explore possibilities

qualitativeQuotes.name: Sandra MarinRuiz
qualitativeQuotes.text: Practitioner partnerships (6 in direct conversation and actionable items), Softlanding (2)

qualitativeQuotes.name: Jorge Tortos
qualitativeQuotes.text: Yes, masstimber from MSU, in Costa Rica there is a need and business opportunity to import sustainable woods

qualitativeQuotes.name: Erick Marín Muller
qualitativeQuotes.text: Yes, underrepresented communities were a significant part of the themes and presentations. Very well organized all around.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alfredo E. Lagleyze
qualitativeQuotes.text: Yes, startup presentation with women and afroamerican

qualitativeQuotes.name: Sandra MarinRuiz
qualitativeQuotes.text: The innovation strategy is inclusive and well designed

qualitativeQuotes.name: Jorge Tortos
qualitativeQuotes.text: After the panelist, I see the capacities in many young entrepreneurs and the impact of partnerships. The culture of cooperation is present in Michigan

qualitativeQuotes.name: Jorge A Valentine
qualitativeQuotes.text: Detroit and Michigan itself are moving forward and harnessing the “yes we can” attitude that can turn towns around. The groups and people we met during yhe tour are testament to that vision and actions.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Alfredo E. Lagleyze
qualitativeQuotes.text: Very well organized and rich in content, congrats!!

qualitativeQuotes.name: Sandra MarinRuiz
qualitativeQuotes.text: I do appreciate the opportunity to be part of ACE. Thanks for all your efforts

qualitativeQuotes.name: Jorge Tortos
qualitativeQuotes.text: A life changing economic experience that involves academia, public sector, the non for profit agile sector and those business oriented under a sustainable lens.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Marcelo Alejandro Collahuazo Taco
qualitativeQuotes.text: I recommend to showcase the entrepreneurs at the beginning or middle of the program.

qualitativeQuotes.name: Erika Martinez
qualitativeQuotes.text: I appreciate you making me part of this program. It has been a self motivated experience. Having the chance of knowing so mano people , learning and sharing experiences is so nice. Besides , understanding what others do to better this world and the way they do it. I would recommed your program. It is gratis you can help us grow and Connecticut with each other
